title: Support GitHub Enterprise Cloud (ghe.com) hosts for PR commands
description: aide hardcodes github.com in remote parsing, API base URLs, and gh CLI calls, so PRs on GHEC data-residency hosts ({company}.ghe.com) are never detected. Thread the host through parsing into GitHubClient: accept github.com and *.ghe.com remotes, derive API base as https://api.{host}, and pass --hostname to gh api. Support both gh CLI and token transports.

title: Add 'aide jira api' raw passthrough subcommand
description:
Add 'aide jira api' — a raw, authenticated REST passthrough for Jira, mirroring 'gh api' ergonomics. Reuses credentials from 'aide login jira' (env var fallback). Intended as a low-level escape hatch for endpoints the typed jira subcommands don't cover and as a tool for agents to script against arbitrary Jira APIs.

## Scope

Single new subcommand: 'aide jira api <endpoint> [flags]'. Out of scope for this epic: analogous api subcommands for ado/github (can be separate epics if/when needed).

## URL handling

- Relative path (e.g., 'rest/api/3/issue/PROJ-1') → prepend scheme+host from configured Jira URL
- Absolute URL accepted only if host matches configured Jira host
- Cross-host absolute URL → reject with clear error (credential-leak guard)
- http:// absolute URL → reject (HTTPS only)
- No automatic API root prepending; caller writes the path. This covers /rest/api/3/, /rest/api/2/, /rest/agile/1.0/, /rest/auth/1/, /rest/servicedeskapi/, etc., uniformly.
- Redirects not followed; 3xx surfaced to caller as-is.

## Flags (mirroring gh api)

- -X, --method <verb>  — HTTP method, default GET
- -f <key=value>       — string field; querystring on GET/HEAD/DELETE, JSON body otherwise
- -F <key=value>       — typed field (numbers, booleans, nulls, @file for file content)
- -H, --header <h>     — extra header; repeatable
- --input <file|->     — raw JSON body from file or stdin

Default Content-Type: application/json for body requests; overridable via -H.

No --paginate in v1. Callers loop via '-f startAt=N -f maxResults=M' themselves (matches raw-passthrough spirit; auto-merging would require Jira-specific response-shape assumptions).

## Auth

- Basic auth from keyring (email + api-token) via 'aide login jira'
- Env var fallback: JIRA_URL / JIRA_EMAIL / JIRA_API_TOKEN
- Same loader as other jira subcommands (probeJiraConfig)

## Output

- Raw response body printed to stdout, unformatted
- No --format flag interaction (passthrough is the whole point)
- 4xx/5xx → print response body, exit non-zero (matches gh)

## Acceptance criteria

- 'aide jira api rest/api/3/myself' returns current-user JSON
- 'aide jira api -X POST rest/api/3/issue -f fields[summary]=Test ...' creates a ticket (or errors cleanly via stdin/--input for nested JSON)
- 'aide jira api https://evil.com/x' is refused with host-mismatch error
- 'aide jira api http://configured.host/x' is refused with HTTPS-only error
- 'echo {...} | aide jira api -X POST --input - rest/api/3/issue' posts stdin body
- 4xx/5xx responses print Jira's error envelope and exit non-zero
- Help text makes clear this is a raw passthrough, not a replacement for typed jira subcommands
close_reason: Implemented and verified: aide jira api passthrough. Pure helpers (resolveEndpoint, parseFields, buildRequest) in src/lib/jira-api.ts with 34 unit tests; command handler in src/cli/commands/jira/api.ts with fetch + redirect: 'manual' + stdin/file --input. Security guards: HTTPS-only on configured Jira host, userinfo stripped, no redirect following. 205/205 tests pass. Branch robb/jira-api-passthrough pushed.

title: Per-host GitHub token auth for ghe.com (token transport)
description: GitHub token storage and GITHUB_TOKEN are host-agnostic: there is one stored token / one env var. For the gh CLI transport this is fine (gh manages per-host auth). But a token-transport user pointed at a {company}.ghe.com remote will send their github.com PAT to api.{company}.ghe.com, which fails with a confusing 401. Add per-host token storage (e.g. 'aide login github --host acme.ghe.com') and/or a clear error when token auth targets a non-github.com host without a host-specific token. Surfaced by adversarial review of ax-c1c (GHE support). Not a credential-leak risk since the host comes from the user's own remote.
